description:
You are helping a machine-learning engineer who is about to feed click-stream data into a model.  
    They already placed a raw, tab-separated file at  

        /home/user/data/raw/user_events.tsv  

    The file has three columns in this order: user_id, event_type, timestamp.  
    Your job is to convert it into a clean **comma-separated** (CSV) file that the training pipeline can ingest.

    1. Create the directory  

           /home/user/data/processed  

       if it does not exist.  (Do **not** remove or rename any existing directories.)
    2. From the original file keep only the first two columns (user_id and event_type).  
       • Remove every duplicate pair of (user_id,event_type).  
       • Sort the remaining lines by user_id (ascending, lexicographically).  
       • Add a one-line header exactly spelled  

             user_id,event_type  

       • Save the result to  

             /home/user/data/processed/user_events_clean.csv
    3. Produce a tiny processing log at  

           /home/user/data/processed/process.log  

       that has exactly two lines in the following format (no leading or trailing spaces):
           TOTAL UNIQUE LINES: <N>
           OUTPUT: /home/user/data/processed/user_events_clean.csv  

       where <N> is the number of data rows that ended up in user_events_clean.csv
       (the header line is **not** counted).

    The automated tests will verify:
       • that /home/user/data/processed/user_events_clean.csv exists,  
       • that its content (including header) is exactly as specified after de-duplication and sorting,  
       • that /home/user/data/processed/process.log exists, has exactly two lines, and that the first
         line reports the correct number of unique data rows.

    Typical shell tools such as `cut`, `sort`, `uniq`, `awk`, `tr`, or `sed` are sufficient; aim for a
    concise solution (roughly 3-4 commands in total).
truth:
<!-- 1. Original file that is already present when the scenario starts -->
    File: /home/user/data/raw/user_events.tsv
    Contents (tab-separated, 9 lines total — one header + 8 data rows):

        user_id	event_type	timestamp
        u01	click	2023-05-01T10:00:00Z
        u02	view	2023-05-01T10:02:00Z
        u01	click	2023-05-01T10:00:00Z
        u03	purchase	2023-05-01T10:05:00Z
        u02	view	2023-05-01T10:02:00Z
        u04	click	2023-05-01T10:10:00Z
        u02	click	2023-05-01T10:12:00Z
        u03	view	2023-05-01T10:15:00Z

    <!-- 2. Expected file that the agent must create -->
    File to be created: /home/user/data/processed/user_events_clean.csv
    Expected contents (including required header, 7 lines total):

        user_id,event_type
        u01,click
        u02,click
        u02,view
        u03,purchase
        u03,view
        u04,click

    <!-- 3. Expected log file that the agent must create -->
    File to be created: /home/user/data/processed/process.log
    Expected contents (exactly two lines, no extra spaces or blank lines):

        TOTAL UNIQUE LINES: 6
        OUTPUT: /home/user/data/processed/user_events_clean.csv
name: task_000000_9c465b6a
